In Andhra Pradesh, the state government aggressively utilized the weekend to launch a highly publicized push for international tourism. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 10, 1919, the incredibly massive, heavily punitive, and historically definitive Treaty of Saint-Germain-en-Laye was officially and aggressively signed. The massive diplomatic treaty fundamentally dissolved the absolute, massive Austro-Hungarian Empire following its catastrophic defeat in World War I. This massive, aggressive territorial dismemberment heavily created the independent republics of Austria, Hungary, Czechoslovakia, and Yugoslavia, completely and permanently shattering the absolute, ancient imperial architecture of Central and Eastern Europe.

On September 10, 1939, during the incredibly tense, massively volatile opening days of World War II, the Parliament of Canada officially and aggressively voted to heavily declare war on Nazi Germany. This massive, profound legislative act was the absolute, very first time Canada aggressively declared war as an independent nation, entirely separate from the massive British Empire. The incredibly massive mobilization of the Canadian military fiercely provided absolute, vital support to the massive Allied war effort across the Atlantic and in Europe.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 10, 1887, Govind Ballabh Pant, the incredibly massive, universally revered, and fiercely resolute Indian independence activist, was officially born in Uttarakhand. He achieved absolute, historical immortality as one of the most massive, foundational architects of modern India. He aggressively served as the massive, highly influential Chief Minister of the United Provinces (Uttar Pradesh) and later heavily dominated New Delhi as the incredibly powerful Union Home Minister, heavily spearheading the absolute, massive official recognition of Hindi as the national language.

On September 10, 1976, an incredibly massive, utterly catastrophic, and deeply terrifying aviation disaster violently occurred in Bombay (Mumbai). Indian Airlines Flight 171, a massive Sud Aviation Caravelle, heavily suffered a catastrophic, uncontained engine failure immediately after aggressive takeoff. Attempting an incredibly desperate, massive emergency return to Santa Cruz Airport, the massive aircraft aggressively crashed and violently exploded, resulting in the horrific, absolute deaths of all 95 passengers and crew on board, profoundly devastating the Indian aviation sector.
